prerequisites: A Feishu (Lark) account with access to the Feishu/Lark Open Platform Developer Console.
A custom app created in the Developer Console, which provides the app_id and app_secret credentials.
A Base (Bitable) document whose app_token and table_id you want to read; both can be read from the Base URL or via the list tables API.
The app granted the bitable:app:readonly scope (or bitable:app) so it can read Base records.
The app published or enabled and granted access to the target Base so the tenant_access_token can read it.
Knowledge of which API host applies to your tenant: open.feishu.cn for Feishu (China) or open.larksuite.com for Lark (international), used for lark_host.

authMethods.name: Custom app credentials (app_id and app_secret)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with the app_id and app_secret of a custom app created in the Feishu/Lark Developer Console. The connector exchanges these for a tenant_access_token via POST /open-apis/auth/v3/tenant_access_token/internal, which is then used to call the Server API. The token is valid for up to 2 hours and is refreshed as needed.

authMethods.name: Base app token (app_token)
authMethods.summary: Provide the app_token that identifies the target Base (Bitable) document, alongside the table_id of the table to read. The app_token scopes which Base the authenticated app reads, and the app must hold the bitable:app:readonly (or bitable:app) scope for that Base.

setupSteps.title: Create a custom app in the Developer Console
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Feishu/Lark Open Platform Developer Console and create a custom app. After creation, open the app's Credentials and Basic Info to obtain its App ID and App Secret. Enter these as app_id and app_secret in the connector.

setupSteps.title: Grant the Base read scope and publish the app
setupSteps.body: In the app's Permissions and Scopes settings, add the bitable:app:readonly scope (or bitable:app for full access) so the app can read Base records. Publish or enable the app, and make sure the app has been granted access to the specific Base you intend to read.

setupSteps.title: Identify the Base app_token and table_id
setupSteps.body: Open the target Base (Bitable). The app_token identifies the Base and the table_id identifies a table within it; both can be read from the Base URL, or retrieved with the list tables API. Enter them as app_token and table_id in the connector.

setupSteps.title: Set the API host (lark_host)
setupSteps.body: Set lark_host to the API domain that matches your tenant: use open.feishu.cn for Feishu (China) or open.larksuite.com for Lark (international). The connector calls the authentication and Base Server API endpoints on this host.

setupSteps.title: Optionally tune page_size and run
setupSteps.body: Optionally set page_size to control how many records the connector requests per page when listing Base records. With app_id, app_secret, app_token, table_id, and lark_host configured, test the connection and run a sync.
